narrative.headline: Mathematics: improving
narrative.summarySentence: FAY All Students Mathematics percent passing changed by about 2.8 percentage points per year across 2022,2023,2024,2025.
narrative.evidenceSentence: Fitted years: 2022,2023,2024,2025. FY2026 indicator 45.0 with likely range 38.0-52.0 (8 of 10 backtested forecasts landed within this band).
narrative.caveatSentence: This is a descriptive trend forecast, not a causal claim about what produced the pattern. The FY2030 value is an illustration of where the school would land if the recent yearly trend simply continued; it is not a decision-ready prediction.
narrative.recommendedReviewPrompt: Review grade-level and subgroup trends before making school support decisions.

narrative.headline: English Language Arts: stable or mixed
narrative.summarySentence: FAY All Students English Language Arts percent passing changed by about 0.5 percentage points per year across 2022,2023,2024,2025.
narrative.evidenceSentence: Fitted years: 2022,2023,2024,2025. FY2026 indicator 48.0 with likely range 41.0-55.0 (8 of 10 backtested forecasts landed within this band).
narrative.caveatSentence: This is a descriptive trend forecast, not a causal claim about what produced the pattern. The FY2030 value is an illustration of where the school would land if the recent yearly trend simply continued; it is not a decision-ready prediction.
narrative.recommendedReviewPrompt: Review grade-level and subgroup trends before making school support decisions.

narrative.headline: Science: improving
narrative.summarySentence: FAY All Students Science percent passing changed by about 3.8 percentage points per year across 2022,2023,2024,2025.
narrative.evidenceSentence: Fitted years: 2022,2023,2024,2025. FY2026 indicator 32.0 with likely range 22.0-42.0 (8 of 10 backtested forecasts landed within this band).
narrative.caveatSentence: This is a descriptive trend forecast, not a causal claim about what produced the pattern. The FY2030 value is an illustration of where the school would land if the recent yearly trend simply continued; it is not a decision-ready prediction.
narrative.recommendedReviewPrompt: Review grade-level and subgroup trends before making school support decisions.
